description:
## What
Add `scheduled_at INTEGER` (nullable Unix ms) column to the posts table via migration. When `POST /api/v1/statuses` receives a `scheduled_at` ISO8601 param that is at least 5 minutes in the future, create the post with `scheduled_at` set and return a `ScheduledStatus` object (not a `Status`). Exclude scheduled (unpublished) posts from all timeline queries, outbox, and delivery queue.

## Why
Clients (Ivory, Elk) use the ScheduledStatus API to let users queue posts for future delivery. Without this, scheduled post UI in those clients silently fails or errors.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: curl mastodon.social POST /api/v1/statuses with a scheduled_at param and capture the exact ScheduledStatus JSON shape returned; check mastodon.social source or docs for the minimum 5-minute enforcement rule; review sqlx migration patterns used in this codebase
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: write SQLite migration adding `scheduled_at INTEGER` nullable column; modify post-creation handler to detect scheduled_at param, validate it is >= now()+5min (return 422 otherwise), create post row with scheduled_at set and published_at NULL; serialize ScheduledStatus response per mastodon.social shape; add WHERE clause to all timeline/outbox queries excluding rows where scheduled_at IS NOT NULL AND published_at IS NULL
- Spawn a Test subagent to: test that POST with scheduled_at in past or < 5min returns 422; test that POST with valid future scheduled_at returns ScheduledStatus not Status; test that scheduled post does not appear in home or public timeline queries

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: mastodon.social POST /api/v1/statuses with scheduled_at param — observe the exact ScheduledStatus JSON shape (fields: id, scheduled_at, params, media_attachments) and replicate; mastodon.social enforces >=5min minimum
- Test approach: unit-test the 5-minute validation function with boundary values; integration test that confirms ScheduledStatus shape matches expected fields; query DB directly to assert scheduled_at is set and published_at is NULL

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths
- Validate scheduled_at is parseable ISO8601 and in the future by at least 5 minutes; return 422 with descriptive error on failure
- Exclude scheduled posts from ALL read paths: home timeline, public timeline, outbox, account statuses endpoint
- Never return a scheduled post as a `Status` object — always `ScheduledStatus`
title: Add scheduled_at column and POST /api/v1/statuses scheduled_at support
